clusters.title: Fumbling firebombers almost set themselves alight in arson attack

items.title: Fumbling firebombers almost set themselves alight in arson attack
items.standfirst: Two men targeted a tobacconist armed with Molotov cocktails in Queensland, nearly setting their own flannel shirts alight.
items.content: A pair of fumbling firebombers have nearly set themselves on fire in an arson attack in Queensland.It's the fourth time the store in Mackay has been targeted and while the pair didn't look like masterminds, police aren't ruling out links to organised crime.The two men targeted a tobacconist armed with Molotov cocktails about midnight on Friday.READ MORE: Groundbreaking Aussie test 'picked up 100 per cent of early ovarian cancers'Their attempts were unsuccessful at first, with the firebombs rebounding.The pair nearly setting their own flannel shirts alight before finally achieving their goal."He's then ignited that and what you can see is a large flash and explosion," Queensland Police Acting Inspector Chris Easton said.That explosion was seen on CCTV cameras across the road and was heard from kilometres away.Neighbouring businesses were also caught in the crossfire, with a family-run repair shop ruined and a gym next door left gutted.READ MORE: Gold Coast mum's pleas to police labelled 'lower priority' before murder "Livelihoods have been, have been ruined if you like," Eaton said."People will have employment loss because of it. So we're taking it incredibly seriously."We know that there were three other similar offences targeted at that particular store over the last four to six weeks."We certainly believe that those four offenses are linked."While no civilians were injured, police suspect one of the two men responsible was and are calling for help to find the firebombers before the next blaze."It's highly likely that offender sustained burns during the incident," Eaton said."We're calling for information of members of the public who may know someone who has sustained some serious burns over the last few days."NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here
